member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ApiVersionDepreciatedMessageHandler.ApiVersionDepreciatedStatusCode
member.summary: The status code returned by the API when the version is no longer supported.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ApiVersionMessageHandler
member.summary: Add the currently used API version to the requests.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotApexTestsAuthenticationMessageHandler
member.summary: A message handler that adds the request headers required for authenticating with the Copilot API with an integration id and HMAC key. This is used when the product is running under automation tests, to get around the need to be signed in to a GitHub account.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotApexTestsAuthenticationMessageHandler._currentGitHubUserId
member.summary: The index of the user id to send in the request. When rate limited, we increment this to switch to the next user id.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotApexTestsAuthenticationMessageHandler.Authenticate(System.Net.Http.Headers.HttpRequestHeaders)
member.summary: Authenticates an outbound request to a copilot endpoint.
member.param: The collection to add authenticating headers to.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotApexTestsAuthenticationMessageHandler.ProcessHmacKey(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the HMAC key to use for authenticating requests.
member.param: The HMAC key. While the key may look like hex-encoded bytes, it is actually an arbitrary string that will be ASCII encoded from chars to bytes (i.e. NOT hex decoded) to get the key.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainer
member.summary: Lazily initializing implementation container for It is not valid to call the client's rpc target until the service has been created (so, ServiceFactory.CreateAsync has returned) But this presents a problem if any of our services need to call the client's rpc target during initialization. Adding this extra layer allows our services to have client rpc dependencies injected.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainer.UITestRequestCacheDirectoryEnvVarName
member.summary: Optional environment variable to specify the directory where the request cache should be found.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainer.UITestFullyQualifiedTestCaseEnvVarName
member.summary: Optional environment variable to specify the test case being run.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainer.UITestDisableSnippyEnvVarName
member.summary: Optional environment variable to specify if Snippy should be disabled.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainer.UITestRequestCacheCacheHashInputsEnvVarName
member.summary: Optional environment variable to specify additional inputs to hash when creating the request cache e.g. "version=1.2;iteration=3".

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainerAssemblyLoadWrapper
member.summary: Outer layer of indirection for constructing the copilot service. Exposes an interface to ServiceFactory which contains no types that reference other Conversations assemblies. This allows ServiceFactory to be loaded even if they are missing, and that lets us send panic telemetry & log the failure to disk.
Creates the service container. Returns an 'object' type so that the ServiceFactory, which references this, can be loaded.
member.returns: A CopilotServiceContainer, but it's an object instead of the specific type.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotServiceContainerAssemblyLoadWrapper.GetServiceDescriptor(Microsoft.ServiceHub.Framework.ServiceMoniker)
member.summary: Gets the service descriptor. Hides the reference to ServiceIdentity from the caller.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.CopilotTokenAuthenticatingMessageHandler.#ctor(Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.CopilotClient.CopilotTokenManagerProxyClient,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: If SKU isolation endpoint needs to be used.
If true GitHub token (gho_*) will be used instead.
If the proxy endpoint needs to be used. The API endpoint will be used by default.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ExperimentalChat.ClarifyResponseIntent
member.summary: The goal of is to take in an ambiguous user interaction and guide the user on how to clarify their request given available context in a natural way. Ultimately relies on the to call it ONLY when there's ambiguity. Once called we're effectively able to educate users on available context's or at least give them an idea of potential areas for exploration.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ExperimentalChat.ClarifyResponseIntent.#ctor(System.String,Conversations.Kernel.ILLMClient,Conversations.Shared.Options.IMarshaledCopilotOptions)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of .
member.param: A natural language description of the ambiguity detected in the request that needs to be clarified.
The client used to talk to an LLM

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ExperimentalChat.RespondIntent
member.summary: The goal of is to take in a user interaction and respond to it in an informed, natural way. Ultimately you can think of this as a "super intent" that encourages the model to follow a certain set of guidelines when responding BUT ultimately relies on to call it ONLY when there's enough information to properly respond.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ExperimentalChat.ResponseIntentSelector
member.summary: The goal of is to take in a user interaction and determine if we need to gain clarification OR if we need to respond to the user directly.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory
member.summary: Constructs the service implementation. If this class throws, normally the only way to tell is looking at ServiceHub logs, and the client will likely be unaware of any issue. We add extra logging to a panic log file in %TEMP%\VSGitHubCopilotLogs\ServiceActivationFailures.log, as well as best-effort telemetry. This class should also not reference any of our other assemblies, or any types that reference them, because if they are missing, this class needs to be loadable or else we will not be able to report the problem.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory.RunId
member.summary: Unique ID for this run of the service.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory.Instance
member.summary: Gets or sets singleton instance of the service, it should only be initialized one time. Not strongly typed so we avoid referencing other assemblies in this class, though that may not be a relevant concern anymore.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory.ServiceProcessOneTimeSetup(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Perform initialization that should only happen once per service process.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory.WritePanicLog(System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Write an exception to the panic log file.
member.returns: The same exception argument passed in, unless writing failed, in which case it'll be an AggregateException that also contains the argument

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.ServiceFactory.PanicInitializationTelemetry(System.String,System.Exception,Microsoft.ServiceHub.Framework.ServiceActivationOptions)
member.summary: Attempt to send telemetry when the service critically fails to initialize. Uses hardcoded constants to avoid external references.

member.name: T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache
member.summary: This class implements a file-based persistent cache for Copilot model requests and responses. Requests are cached based on a hash of the API endpoint and the JSON request payload. The file structure on disk is {cacheRootDirectory}/{sha256(apiUrl + requestPayload)}.json.

member.name: F:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.CacheVersion
member.summary: Bump this version number to force a cache miss and repopulate the cache with fresh requests.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.AdditionalHashInputs
member.summary: Additional metadata to include in the hash for each request. Also logged in the cache file for reference.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.AdditionalHashInputsSorted
member.summary: A sorted version of . Used to ensure consistent hash computation.

member.name: P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.AdditionalMetadataToStore
member.summary: Additional metadata to store on any items that are added to the cache. Does not affect the hash computation.

members.member:
Initializes a new instance of the class.
The root directory for the cache, independent of test iteration. The test iteration. Each test iteration stores independent/unique cached content within the cache. Additional inputs to include in the hash for each request. /// Additional metadata to include on any items that are added to the cache. The logger to use. Indicates whether to use request cache files that are considered stale (i.e. older than 28 days).

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.GetMetrics
member.summary: Gets and logs the current cache metrics.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.GetCacheFilePath(System.String,System.Uri)
member.summary: Get the path on disk where a given request should be located. The path is based on a hash of the API endpoint and the HTTP request payload, which is typically a JSON string. The API endpoint is included in the hash in order to support testing new API endpoints with the same payload, which would otherwise collide in the cache.
member.param: The Uri to the HTTP endpoint where the request is being made.
member.returns: The path on disk that should be used for caching this request.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.CombineMetadata(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Returns the full metadata dictionary to use, combining cache entry-specific metadata with any additional global metadata for the cache.
member.param: The optional cache entry-specific metadata dictionary to use.
member.returns: The combined metadata dictionary to persist.
member.remarks: If the same metadata key exists in both global and entry-specific metadata dictionaries, the entry-specific one takes precedence.

member.name: M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.Conversations.Service.TestCopilotRequestCache.NormalizeEscapedLineEndings(System.String)
member.summary:
This is a hack to normalize escaped line endings in the request payload since the Copilot extension generates a lot of model inputs with Environment.NewLine, which is 
 on Unix and 
 on Windows. Those new line endings end up as escaped "" and "
" strings in the HTTP request JSON payloads. In order to have cache hits across both platforms, we need to normalize line endings on the request input before hashing it.
member.param: The input which might have mixed line endings.
member.returns: The input with normalized line endings.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.
member.remarks: This definition is provided by the IsExternalInit NuGet package (https://www.nuget.org/packages/IsExternalInit). Please see https://github.com/manuelroemer/IsExternalInit for more information.

member.name: M:Conversations.Shared.RetryAuthHandler.SendAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Checks if response had an auth error. If yes, refreshes the Copilot token and retries the request.
